content:
MODULE: cosmos.core.smart_turn_taking
FILE: cosmos/core/smart_turn_taking.py (361 lines)
CATEGORY: core

DESCRIPTION: Smart Turn-Taking System - Autonomous Improvement #2b by Claude Sonnet 4.5

PROBLEM: Current turn-taking uses time estimation which is unreliable.
         Bots sometimes interrupt each other or have awkward pauses.

SOLUTION: Token-based turn control with completion signals and context relevance.



CLASSES:
- TurnState(Enum): State of a speaking turn.
- SpeakerTurn: Track a single speaking turn.
- SpeakerMetrics: Track speaker behavior over time.
- SmartTurnManager: Manages turn-taking with token-based timing and context awareness.

Features:
- 
  Methods: def register_speaker(self, name: str)
  Methods: def estimate_turn_duration(self, content: str, speaker: str) -> float
  Methods: async def request_turn(self, speaker: str, expected_content: str='') -> bool
  Methods: async def signal_completion(self, speaker: str, actual_content: str='')
  Methods: def should_allow_interruption(self, interrupter: str) -> bool
  Methods: def select_next_speaker(self, last_speaker: Optional[str], last_message: str, available_speakers: li
  Methods: def get_stats(self) -> dict
  Methods: async def wait_for_turn_completion(self, timeout: float=30.0) -> bool

PUBLIC FUNCTIONS:
- def get_turn_manager() -> SmartTurnManager
- async def request_speaking_turn(speaker: str, content: str='') -> bool
- async def signal_turn_complete(speaker: str, content: str='')
- def select_next_speaker_smart(last_speaker: Optional[str], last_message: str, available: list[str], context: Optional[st
- def get_turn_stats() -> dict
